pages.text: $(o)"The first circles were not on mountaintops, nor underground — but on ordinary grass, because the first mages were ordinary people."$()$(br2)$(l)Initiate Circle Ruins$() dot the plains and forests — the entry-tier markers of the lost civilization.

pages.title: Structure
pages.text: A $(l)Tier 1 Dormant$() circle. The center holds an $(l)Ancient Circle Stone$(); four $(l)Lesser Rune Stones$() and four more $(l)Ancient Circle Stones$() form the simplest 3×3 layout.$(br2)Usually overgrown — grass, vines, flowers must be cleared to read the patterns.

pages.title: Loot
pages.text: Centers and corners typically hold:$(br)· One $(l)life-class$() scroll (Ordered Vault / Thousand-League Return / Oreblood Revelation)$(br)· A few $(l)rift fragments$()$(br)· One or two stray $(l)mana crystals$()$(br2)Some cores have been claimed by wildlife — bees especially.

pages.title: Why the Plains
pages.text: The old initiation rule: $(l)the first circle must be built where it can be seen$().$(br2)Designed to teach new mages that "magic is public" — not hidden in mountains, not buried below, but coexisting with daily life.$(br2)$(o)"A mage is a neighbor first."$() — Initiate's Oath
